steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined clause: "helping in turn to sustain several other species that rely on them as a food source." The clause follows a claim that solving the spawning mystery will lead to better conservation of glass eels. We need to identify what role this added clause plays in the text's argument.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It presents a finding from a study that identifies the circumstances required to ensure the survival of glass eels.': No study is mentioned. The passage presents what scientists believe, and the underlined clause says nothing about conditions for eel survival. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It suggests that scientists are more concerned about other species than about glass eels' habitat.': The passage never implies scientists care more about other species. The underlined clause adds to the conservation benefit; it doesn't shift concern away from eels. ✗
  • Choice C — 'It indicates that the benefit of understanding glass eels' spawning behavior extends beyond the eels.': Exactly right. The clause introduces other species that depend on glass eels as prey, widening the conservation payoff beyond the eels themselves. ✓
  • Choice D — 'It discusses a role that glass eels and other species serve in supporting the ecosystem of the Sargasso Sea.': The clause is about benefits of conservation research, not a general ecological role of eels in the Sargasso Sea. The Sargasso Sea is not even referenced in the underlined portion. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
